lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You move quietly. I almost didn't hear you.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: That was the idea. I was watching the grain store.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I already cleared it. Three rats since dawn.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I know. I saw. You fight like you've got something to prove.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Maybe I do. This guild doesn't build itself.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...No. But you don't have to build it alone.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Do you ever rest? You've been at the board since first light.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Resting is how you lose what you've built.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Resting is how you keep it. Listen to the rain for a moment.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Fine. One moment.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: The sound of water on stone. That's what home sounds like.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Home. I haven't thought of anywhere that way in a long time.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: When the plague rats came, you didn't run.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Neither did you. Though you had more reason to.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You built this from nothing. You had the most to lose. And you wouldn't let anyone stand in front of you.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I don't know how to be stood in front of. I never have.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Then we'll learn it the same way you taught me to fight. Slowly. Badly at first.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...All right. Slowly.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've mapped the courier routes. Three optimal paths to the monastery.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The monks' cat told me something is wrong in the scriptorium. I can feel it.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Feel it. You can feel it.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Mock if you like. My whiskers know things your maps don't.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I'm not mocking. I'm... cataloguing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That noble's letter you delivered. It smelled like ash and lavender.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: It was sealed. You shouldn't have been sniffing it.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Ash means grief. Lavender means someone wants to forget.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Or someone was burning herbs near their writing desk.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Possibly. But my way is more interesting, isn't it?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: ...Annoyingly, yes.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've been thinking about what you said. About the omens.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: A formal cat like you, thinking about omens?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Your intuitions have been right more often than chance allows. That's... data.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: And your routes have kept us safe every time. That's a kind of wisdom too.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Perhaps the guild needs both. The map and the whisker.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Now you're talking sense.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You're fast. Faster than any cat I've seen.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Speed keeps you alive when you're small.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I wouldn't know about small. But I can hold a door all night.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: That's... actually useful. The granary gate doesn't latch properly.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Show me. I'll keep it shut.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Why did you come here? You could guard any barn in the county.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Barns don't need protecting. They just need sitting in.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And a guild does?

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: A guild has cats who care about each other. That's worth more than grain.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You're more thoughtful than you look.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Big body, big heart. It's a package deal.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: The plague rats almost took the guildhall. I held the door, but barely.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I saw the scratches. You should have called for help.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You were clearing the cathedral. The town needed you more.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: The guild needs you too. Don't forget that.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I won't. As long as you remember you don't have to carry everything alone.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I learned to. When you're a stray, the only paws you can count on are your own.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You're not a stray anymore. Try unlearning it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...I'll try.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You were at the warehouse before dawn. I saw your paw prints.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I prefer working before the town wakes. Fewer distractions.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Efficient. I respect that.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: And you always deliver letters on time. The monks have noticed.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Punctuality is the simplest form of respect.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've been tracking our earnings. You consistently outperform on pest control.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I don't do it for the numbers.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I know. That's what makes the numbers so interesting.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You see patterns in everything, don't you?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Someone has to. Otherwise it's just cats chasing mice.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Sometimes chasing mice is enough.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You never ask where I go at night.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You always come back. That's sufficient.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Most cats would be suspicious.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Suspicion is a waste of energy spent on cats who've already proven themselves.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...That might be the kindest thing anyone's said to me.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Don't tell the Siamese. I have a reputation.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You're so quiet I sometimes forget you're in the room.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: And you're so loud I never forget.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Loud! I'm expressive. There's a difference.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: If you say so.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I do say so. Frequently. And at volume.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You were on the roof again last night. I could hear you singing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It wasn't singing. It was... communing.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: With the moon?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: With whatever listens. Don't you ever feel something watching back?

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...Sometimes. In the grain store, when it's very still.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: See? You understand more than you let on.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I had a vision about you. Don't laugh.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I'm not laughing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You were standing in a great hall. Sunlight through stained glass. Cats everywhere.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: That sounds like the guildhall. On a good day.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It was the guildhall. Years from now. And you were still here.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Then I suppose I'd better stay.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Sorry about the door frame. I keep misjudging my shoulders.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You took out two boards. I measured.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I'll fix them. I'm good with wood.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You're good with everything that doesn't require fitting through normal-sized openings.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Fair point.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The dock foreman asked for you specifically. Said you're the only cat who intimidates the ship rats.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I don't try to intimidate anyone.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: That's exactly why it works. Genuine presence, not performance.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: You'd know about performance. You walk like you're always being watched.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Because I usually am. Observation is a two-way street.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I just walk. People get out of the way on their own.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: When the plague hit the docks, you had the evacuation planned in minutes.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And you carried three kittens out while the water rose. Plans don't do that.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Someone has to carry things. It's what I'm for.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You're not 'for' anything. You choose to help. That matters more.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: ...Thank you. That's not something big cats hear often.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Then people aren't paying attention.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Hold still. I'm reading your aura.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: My what now?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Your energy. It's... remarkably calm. Like a warm hearth.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I've been told I run warm. Thick fur.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It's more than fur. You make other cats feel safe just by being nearby.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: That story you told the kittens about Saint Gertrude. Was any of it true?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: All of it was true. Some of it happened.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: That doesn't make sense.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Truth and facts are cousins, not twins. The kittens understood.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: They also believe I'm part bear.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Are you sure you're not?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I dreamed the guild burned. I woke up terrified.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: It won't burn. I check the hearth every night.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Every night since I came here. Old habit from the barns.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Then my dream was wrong. That's the first time.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Maybe it wasn't wrong. Maybe it was a warning, and I already answered it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You reorganized the job board.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: By difficulty, then by reward ratio. It was chaos before.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: It was my chaos. I knew where everything was.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Did you though?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Fine. It's better now.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The other cats look to you. You know that.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: They shouldn't. I'm just the first one who showed up.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: That's exactly why they look to you. First through the door, last to leave.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Someone has to be.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And someone has to make sure the leader doesn't burn out. That's my job.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I didn't assign you that.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: When I started this guild, I thought strength was all you needed.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And now?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Now I think you need someone who can count the fish while someone else catches them.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: A crude metaphor. But accurate.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: It was hard, you know. Letting you take the ledger. I kept wanting to look over your shoulder.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I noticed. You stopped after the third week.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Stay. The guild needs your kind of strength too. I can't be every kind.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I'm not going anywhere. Someone has to keep the ledger. And remind the founder it's allowed to put things down.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The saints sent you here. I'm sure of it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: A storm sent me here. And an empty stomach.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Same thing, from the right angle.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: That's not how angles work.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: It is from mine.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You talk to the chapel window. I've seen you.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I talk to Saint Gertrude. Whether she answers is between us.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Does she?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: She told me the plague would end. And it did.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: We ended it. With claws and sweat.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Maybe the claws were her answer.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Do you remember the night you arrived? The lightning?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I remember being cold and hungry and alone.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I saw the lightning from the bell tower. One bolt, straight down into the market square.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That's where I found you the next morning. Exactly where the bolt struck.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...Coincidence. I've never been chosen by anything. I don't know how to be.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Maybe 'chosen' is the wrong word. Maybe it just means we chose to follow. The saints don't have to do all the work.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...That, I can almost believe.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: A beautiful coincidence either way.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: We should talk about where this guild is headed.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: We've come a long way from that first night in the storm.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The ledger agrees. Fish reserves are stable. Operations are... functional.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Functional? We're a family. The saints didn't bring us together for bookkeeping.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: She's right. I didn't stay for the fish. I stayed for all of you.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Then we agree. This guild is more than a business. It's a home.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: ...Home. Yes. That's what this is.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: The plague is behind us. The town is recovering.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I still smell them in the walls sometimes. The rats.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The granary stocks are rebuilding. Three weeks behind schedule, but improving.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: I dreamed the procession again. Saint Rosalia's bones through the streets.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: We did what the saints asked. We drove them out.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Together. Every one of us. That's what made the difference.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Next time, I want to be at the front. Not guarding the door.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: There won't be a next time. I've seen it.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Is it true? A feast?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've allocated the budget. Three whole salmon.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Three! I could cry.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You will cry. You cry at everything.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That's because I feel things, unlike some cats I could name.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Enough. Tonight we eat well. Tomorrow we work again. But tonight — we celebrate.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: To the guild!

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: To the guild.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I need to say something. And I need everyone to hear it.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: The jobs we've been taking. The lockboxes. The sabotage. That's not why I joined.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The saints don't guide thieves. I've felt it — the silence where their voices used to be.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: The ledger doesn't lie. We're earning more than ever. But the bonds between us are fraying.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I stayed because this was a family. If it's just a business, I can guard any barn.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...I hear you. All of you. Maybe we lost our way.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: It's not too late to choose differently. It never is.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: There's something at the door. A basket.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Fish! And dried herbs. And... is that a blanket?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: There's a note. 'For the guild that guards our town. With thanks.' No signature.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: The baker's wife. I recognize her handwriting.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: We didn't do this for gifts.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: No. But they gave them anyway. That's what trust looks like.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: This is better than fish. This is home.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You beat the Silver Paws. I didn't think anyone could.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You were one of them.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I was. They promised glory. They delivered politics.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And now you're here because...?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Because you fight for something real. I want to see what that looks like from the inside.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: You've been pacing since dawn. Sit down.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I can't. My legs need to move. My mind needs problems.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Then take a job. The docks need patrol.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Already did that. And the market sweep. Before breakfast.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: ...You're going to be trouble, aren't you?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: The best kind.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I dreamed of the Silver Paws last night. Their hall. Their rules.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Do you miss it?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I miss the certainty. Someone always told you what to do.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: I never had anyone to tell me what to do either. I'm not sure I'd know how to take an order if you gave me one.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Funny. We're broken in opposite directions.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Maybe that's why this works. You're learning to choose. I'm learning to let someone else.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Worth every fish.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Must you knock over everything when you enter a room?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I didn't knock anything over. That barrel was already tilting.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: It was perfectly stable until you ran past it.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You need to relax. Life's too short to worry about barrels.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: How do you just... sit there? For hours?

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: I'm listening. The walls tell you things if you're patient.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: The walls told me I was bored, so I went hunting.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: And yet you came back to sit with me.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: ...The quiet is nice. Sometimes.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: You noticed the rat nest before anyone. How?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Scratches on the floor. Changed scent near the wall. You would have heard it eventually.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Eventually isn't soon enough when lives are at stake.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: That's why you need someone fast. And I need someone who remembers to check twice.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: We make a good team, don't we?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: You solved that puzzle in six moves. The record was twelve.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I see patterns. Can't help it. Everything is a puzzle to me.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Interesting. I see people. Every face is a story.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Which is more useful?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Depends on whether the problem has claws.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I heard you figured out the merchant was lying before anyone else.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: His left ear twitched. Classic tell.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: I noticed the ledger numbers didn't add up. Same conclusion, different path.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Are we competing?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Only if you're losing.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Do you ever feel like you see too much? Every flaw, every angle?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Every day. The Silver Paws used that. Made me their strategist. Their weapon.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: And here?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Here they ask what I think, not just what I see. There's a difference.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: That's called trust. Guard it carefully.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: You filed your patrol report... on the back of a fish wrapper.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: The information is all there. Route, time, findings.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: On a fish wrapper.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Would you prefer I didn't file one at all?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Your filing system is incredible. Every job, every outcome, cross-referenced.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: Was that... a compliment?

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: Don't let it go to your head. I'm just saying — the Silver Paws had nothing like this.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: That's why they lost.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I've been thinking. Your instincts and my systems — we could restructure the job board.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You want to work together? You, who color-codes everything?

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: And you, who solves problems before I finish describing them. Yes.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: The guild would run like a machine.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: A well-organized machine.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You're... larger than I expected.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: And you're faster. I saw you clear the fence.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: In the Silver Paws, speed was everything. Size meant slow.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: In this guild, we don't have to be just one thing.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: That crate was stuck. I couldn't budge it. You moved it with one shoulder.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: And you found the three behind it that I would have missed.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: We're covering each other's weaknesses. That's... new for me.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: That's what a guild is supposed to be.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You never raise your voice. Even when things go wrong.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Shouting just makes everyone smaller. Including you.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: The Silver Paws' leader shouted constantly. I thought that was leadership.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: It's not. Leadership is making others feel bigger than they are.

lines.speaker: bengal
lines.text: You'd have been a terrible Silver Paw. Too kind.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: I'll take that as the highest compliment.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: They're gone. The Silver Paws have left the town.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: I counted their wagons leaving at dawn. Seven cats, three carts. They were well-funded.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: And we beat them with... what? Stubbornness?

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Purpose. They had money. We had something worth fighting for.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Do you think they'll come back?

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: It doesn't matter. We'll be ready.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: Together.

lines.speaker: russian_blue
lines.text: Brother Aldric is gone. The monks packed their ledgers this morning.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: Five days of being watched. I don't think I've ever cleaned my whiskers so much.

lines.speaker: tuxedo
lines.text: It wasn't about whiskers. It was about what we do. Who we are.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: And now the Church knows.

lines.speaker: maine_coon
lines.text: For better or worse.

lines.speaker: siamese
lines.text: The town was watching too. Not just the Inquisitor. Everyone.

lines.speaker: wildcat
lines.text: Then they saw us at our truest. That's all we can offer.
